title: bryophyta
intro: The division Bryophyta is a taxonomic rank that encompasses mosses and other non-vascular plants. It is a division of land plants that typically reproduce via spores and lack true leaves, roots, and stems. Bryophyta division includes a wide range of species such as the common moss, Sphagnum, and the liverwort, Marchantiophyta. Other divisions include Monocotyledonae, which comprises plants with one cotyledon, such as grasses and lilies, Magnoliopsida, a division of flowering plants including roses and apples, and Polypodiopsida, a division of ferns.

classes.class_desc: Sphagnopsida is a class of mosses commonly known as peat mosses, characterized by their ability to thrive in acidic, oxygen-poor environments.
